_doc: Convoy rules (movement-realism branch, PLAN-convoys.md §5). Folded additively into datasets.convoys by build-data. First matching rule wins; a lane matched by no rule never convoys. match.system tests the lane's folded flow system; match.war fires only when a belligerent war covers the lane's theatre. match.flags (optional) restricts a rule to those allegiances. size is [min,max] TOTAL sail in the sim-scale group (the historical sizes are far larger — see research/ambient-flows.md §1 / PLAN-convoys.md §1). escort: 'always' | 'war' (only in an active belligerent war) | 'never'. Every rule carries an evidence class + note (the charter's no-undeclared-estimates rule applied to sim rules). Middle-Passage and framing (coerced-flow) lanes are excluded in the sim, never here.

rules.note: The Carrera flota, compulsory by ordinance 1564 to the free-trade decree; a capitana/almiranta galleon pair escorts. Historical fleets ran 17–50+ sail; the sim draws a spectator-scale group.
The Carrera flota (older lane id); as carrera-de-indias.
The one grouped annual Pacific convoy timed to the Portobelo fair (the Armada del Mar del Sur bringing Potosí silver up to Panama).
The Brazil frotas, compulsory 1649 to Sept 1765, under 1–2 crown warships; historical sizes 20–100 sail (asserted).
The Portuguese Carreira da Índia sailed in annual armadas from Lisbon round the Cape; grouping strongest in the 16th–early 17th c.
The Carreira da Índia (older lane id); as carreira-da-india.
VOC return-fleet cohorts on the Cape route; 2–3 outward and 1–3 homeward fleets a year, escorted on the European legs in wartime.
The English Levant Company's Smyrna convoy. French échelles shipping (individual tramping, the caravane maritime) and Venetian trade stay single-ship.
Wartime trade convoy on a belligerent's lanes; the British Convoy Acts (1793/1798/1803) made it mandatory. Post-1793 British convoyed share is asserted 0.75–0.95 (wide — verify vs Knight).
